marketState: neutral-to-bearish longer term, tactically supported near-term by hot weather
summary: NG front trades at $2.927, essentially at modeled fair value ($2.917) with no meaningful mispricing edge in either direction. Seasonality is weak (price -10.7pp below the typical-year path, -14.1% YTD vs -3.4% median) and low-reliability history favors a modest further pullback over the next month (typical 4-week move -3.1%, prob-higher only 31-38% except week 3 at 50%). The standout bullish input is weather: cooling demand is running +23.5% above the 5-year norm and near the top of its range (rangePos 0.92) over the next week, moderating to +10.1% over days 8-16, which should support prompt prices near-term even as storage carries a +184 Bcf surplus vs 5-year and a bearish +72 Bcf build surprise. Managed money is net short but only at the 35th percentile (not an extreme, crowded position), and daily/weekly technicals show a bearish trend structure (EMA50<200, negative MACD) with RSI nearing oversold (36-38), arguing against chasing strength. Net-net: structurally capped by oversupply and soft seasonality into autumn, but the current heat wave offers a tactical bounce window before the broader downtrend likely reasserts.

longTerm.rationale: Storage sits +184 Bcf above the 5y average with a bearish +72 Bcf surprise build, seasonality is weak and low-reliability (typical 4w move -3.1%, prob-higher <50% in 3 of 4 weeks), and daily/weekly technicals confirm a bearish trend (EMA50<200, negative MACD) — together outweighing the moderating 8-16D weather support (+10.1% vs 5y, down from +23.5% near-term).
longTerm.invalidation: A daily close and hold above $3.10, a bullish storage surprise (miss vs forecast), or 8-16D weather demand re-accelerating back toward +20%+ vs 5y would invalidate the bearish thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: 1-7D cooling demand is +23.5% above the 5-year norm and near the top of its range (rangePos 0.92) and still rising (+11.9 24h), while front price sits only at fair value with a slight front-over-next backwardation (-$0.035) confirming near-term tightness — a setup for a weather-driven bounce even though the daily/weekly trend remains technically bearish.
shortTerm.invalidation: A sharp cooling in the 1-7D forecast, price closing below $2.84, or the front-next spread flipping to contango would invalidate this near-term long.

keyDrivers: Near-term weather demand +23.5% vs 5-year, near top of range (0.92) and rising — bullish for prompt prices
Storage surplus +184 Bcf vs 5-year with a +72 Bcf bearish build surprise — persistent oversupply caps upside
Seasonality weak and low-reliability: prob-higher <50% in 1/2/4 weeks, typical 4-week move -3.1%

Front price sits essentially at fair value ($2.917 vs $2.927), with fair value drifting up to $3.051 by +21D as winter premium builds
risks: Weather forecasts can flip quickly; a cooling trend or reduced heat risk could rapidly remove near-term demand support
